Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: rename /analyze-frame to /livekit-analyze — was shadowed by old endpoint

ROOT CAUSE FOUND: app/routers/analyze.py had an OLD /api/analyze-frame endpoint
(requires image_base64 + auth) mounted BEFORE the livekit_token router. FastAPI
matched the OLD endpoint first, returning 422 on every call. The agent's vision
API calls were silently failing with validation errors this entire time.

- Renamed to /api/livekit-analyze to avoid the route conflict
- Updated agent _analyze_via_api URLs to use /livekit-analyze
- Updated frontend vision handler URL to use /livekit-analyze
- Fixed error message: "my camera's acting up" instead of implying user error
- Added prompt rule: NEVER say "point your phone" — camera is always running

commit.message:
feat: Add comprehensive automated code review system and specialized agents

## Major Additions

### Automated Git Code Review System
- **git-code-reviewer agent**: Automated code quality enforcement on git push
- **Automated review workflow**: Python script with static analysis, security scanning, NFL pattern validation
- **Git hooks integration**: pre-commit (blocking) and post-commit (tracking) hooks
- **Quality metrics tracking**: Historical trend analysis in .claude/review_history/
- **Auto-simplification**: Automatic application of /simplify command to complex functions

### Specialized Development Agents
- **data-engineer agent**: Expert guidance for Databricks, AWS S3, modern data stack best practices
- **data-modeler agent**: Comprehensive data modeling for NFL game prediction with MCP research capabilities

### NFL Game Prediction Data Model
- **Complete data model**: 200+ ML features for NFL game prediction targeting 65%+ accuracy
- **Data dictionary**: Comprehensive table definitions, data types, relationships, validation rules
- **Implementation guide**: 8-week phased roadmap for production deployment

### Fantasy Football System (Complete Pipeline)
- **Player analytics**: Usage metrics, opponent rankings, rolling averages, game script indicators
- **Projection engine**: Weekly + preseason projections with configurable scoring (PPR/Half-PPR/Standard)
- **Draft optimizer**: Interactive CLI with ADP comparison, VORP, positional scarcity alerts
- **Scoring calculator**: Flexible fantasy point calculation engine

### Project Skills & MCP Integration
- **5 Claude Code skills**: /ingest, /weekly-pipeline, /validate-data, /test, /draft-prep
- **Enhanced MCP configuration**: DuckDB, fetch, Sleeper APIs for ADP and validation

## Technical Improvements
- **Enhanced Bronze layer**: 5 new player data types (weekly, snap counts, injuries, rosters, seasonal)
- **Silver layer pipeline**: Complete player transformation with advanced analytics
- **Gold layer projections**: Production-ready weekly and preseason projection system
- **Updated documentation**: CLAUDE.md, copilot-instructions.md, development_tasks.md

## Quality Assurance
- **Automated code review**: Every commit now gets quality analysis with auto-fixes
- **NFL domain validation**: Project-specific rules for team validation, business logic, S3 patterns
- **Security scanning**: Prevents credential exposure and injection vulnerabilities
- **Performance optimization**: Identifies inefficient pandas operations and memory patterns

This update transforms the project from a basic data pipeline into a production-grade fantasy football projection system with automated code quality enforcement.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix compute_loss() method signature compatibility

Fix TypeError: SFTTrainer.compute_loss() got an unexpected keyword argument 'num_items_in_batch'

In transformers 4.36+, the compute_loss() method signature changed to include
an optional num_items_in_batch parameter. Updated SFTTrainer.compute_loss() to
accept this parameter with a default value for backwards compatibility.

Also updated compat.py documentation to include this API change.

provides direct serial access on TCP:9999. No more scraping JRNY
logs for workout data.

commit.message:
fix: resolve Tailwind content paths relative to config file (#98)

The build:css script runs from packages/react/ but the content path
in tailwind.config.lib.js was relative (./packages/react/src/**),
which Tailwind v3 resolves against CWD — not the config file.
This caused zero utility classes to be compiled into dist/styles.css,
forcing consumers to add @source directives and color token mappings.

Use import.meta.url to make the content path absolute so it resolves
correctly regardless of CWD.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Require frontmatter name field for publish (#94)

## Summary
- **Backend**: `validateFrontmatterName` now rejects publish if the
`name` field is missing or doesn't match the slug (previously only
checked mismatch, allowed missing)
- **HTTP handlers**: Skills, roles, and agents all return 400 with a
clear error when `name` is absent
- **Frontend**: Upload form auto-injects `name: <slug>` into frontmatter
before publishing if the field is missing

## Test plan
- [ ] Publish a skill/role/agent with `name` in frontmatter — should
succeed
- [ ] Publish via web UI with no `name` in frontmatter — should
auto-inject and succeed
- [ ] Publish via API with no `name` in frontmatter — should return 400
with clear error
- [ ] Publish with mismatched `name` — should return 400
- [ ] Unit tests pass (`npx vitest run
convex/lib/publishValidation.test.ts`)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: actor system — actorOps sub-opcodes, roomOps fade, currentRoom ordering

Three bytecode parsing bugs prevented actor state from populating:

1. actorOps sub-opcode dispatch was 0-based but SCUMM v5 uses 1-based
   (0x01=setCostume, not 0x00). Stream desync after first sub-opcode.

2. roomOps sub 0x0A mapped to roomScroll (2 params) instead of roomFade
   (1 param). Over-read 2 bytes at ENCD offset 0x005F, desyncing the
   entire bytecode stream — conditionals and putActor never reached.

3. currentRoom was set AFTER startEncdSlot, so putActor stored room=0.
   Moved assignment before ENCD start.

Also: isSoundRunning stub now returns 1 to prevent ENCD from falling
through to an unterminated soundKludge at the script boundary.

Actor 1 (Guybrush) now correctly has room=1, costume=1, visible=1.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: add multi-face card support with tabs, inline display, and MDFC land annotation (#60)

Store per-face data (layout, cardFaces) from Scryfall and render both faces
in the deck list: tabs for dual-image layouts (transform, modal_dfc, battle)
and inline stacked display for shared-image layouts (adventure, split, flip).
Combined oracle text from all faces enables existing analysis modules (tags,
synergy, creature types) to detect abilities on either face with zero code
changes. MDFC land count annotation appears on the mana curve chart.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

637 (#638)

* feat: add Zstd sequence validity specs (Zip/Spec/ZstdSequence.lean)

Define ValidSequence, ValidSequenceList, and ValidOffsetHistory predicates
for Zstd sequence execution (RFC 8878 §3.1.1.4-5). Prove resolveOffset
preserves history size and returns positive offsets. The executeSequences
output length theorem is deferred (monadic for-loop unfolding).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* doc: progress entry for PR #608 rebase (ZstdSequence spec)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: prove decompressRLEBlock and decompressRawBlock correctness

Add three characterizing correctness theorems for Zstd block
decompression functions:
- decompressRawBlock_size: output has exactly blockSize bytes
- decompressRLEBlock_size: output has exactly blockSize bytes
- decompressRLEBlock_content: every output byte equals the source byte

These are tier 2 (characterizing property) specs that describe the
mathematical relationship between inputs and outputs, independent of
implementation. They are building blocks for compositional verification
of the block decompression loop.

Closes #637

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
Fix: prevent duplicate activity events with unique constraint + upsert logic

- Add migration: UNIQUE constraint on (user_id, type, domain) in activity_events
  prevents any duplicate events from being inserted by the database
- /api/crawl/full/start: use .upsert() for both project_created and audit_started
  events, making duplicate inserts idempotent
- /api/crawl/full/next: use .upsert() for both audit_completed code paths,
  prevents race condition between page_limit and queue_empty checks
- /api/projects DELETE: use .upsert() for project_deleted event, makes
  double-click deletion idempotent (only one event created)

With unique constraint in place, duplicate inserts fail gracefully with
ignoreDuplicates: true — no errors, no duplicate rows created.

Run the DB cleanup SQL in Supabase to remove existing duplicates:
  DELETE FROM public.activity_events WHERE domain IS NULL OR domain = '' OR domain = 'undefined';
  DELETE FROM public.activity_events WHERE id NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT ON (user_id, type, domain) id FROM public.activity_events ORDER BY user_id, type, domain, created_at DESC);

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

v1.2.6-beta.1 — optional pill backgrounds for label and value text

Adds Background toggle, Background Color picker, and Transparency slider
to both the Labels and Values format cards. When enabled, each text element
is wrapped in a pill-shaped SVG rect (rx = height/2) sized via getBBox()
after DOM insertion. minRibbonHeight and effectiveNodeWidth both expand
automatically to accommodate the pill so no clipping occurs.

commit.message:
Add dual Hebbian: attention output patches alongside residual patches

Attention patches modify self_attn output before the residual connection,
enabling control over information routing — not just residual stream content.
Each patched layer gets both a DifferentiablePatch (residual, d=256) and an
AttentionPatch (attention output, d=64) with independent ActivationHebbianRules.

New classes: AttentionPatch, MACHDualHebbian, DualHebbianPatchedModel
New flag: --dual-hebbian
~920K trainable params, ~6.5M buffers
